note_data.title: Reactions of organic compounds
keyPoints: **Ethanol** undergoes oxidation with acidified potassium dichromate(VI) to form ethanoic acid
**Carboxylic acids** react with alcohols to form esters and water in the presence of concentrated sulfuric acid
**Esterification** is a condensation reaction because a small molecule (water) is eliminated
**Ethanoic acid** reacts with sodium carbonate to produce sodium ethanoate, carbon dioxide and water
**Carboxylic acids** react with metals to produce a salt and hydrogen gas
**Esters** can be hydrolysed by heating with dilute acid or alkali to form carboxylic acid and alcohol
**Characteristic reaction** of alcohols is oxidation to form carboxylic acids when heated with acidified K₂Cr₂O₇
**Fermentation** of glucose produces ethanol and carbon dioxide using yeast as a catalyst
**Ethanol** burns in air with a clean blue flame to produce carbon dioxide and water

definitions.definition: The reaction between a carboxylic acid and an alcohol to form an ester and water
The breakdown of glucose by yeast in the absence of air to produce ethanol and carbon dioxide
The breakdown of a compound by reaction with water
The addition of oxygen or removal of hydrogen from a compound

howToAnswer.instruction: Give the products only. No equations or explanations needed.
Give the reaction + reason using 'because' or 'this is because'
Include state symbols and balance correctly for full marks

modelAnswer.question: Ethanol can be oxidised to form ethanoic acid. Name the oxidising agent used and state the colour change observed during this reaction.
modelAnswer.answer: Oxidising agent: acidified potassium dichromate(VI) or K₂Cr₂O₇/H₂SO₄. Colour change: orange to green.
markBreakdown: Mark 3: orange to green colour change

commonMistakes: Never say 'potassium dichromate' alone — always say 'acidified potassium dichromate(VI)'
Students often write 'ethyl ethanoate' instead of drawing the structural formula correctly
Don't confuse hydrolysis with hydrogenation — hydrolysis involves breaking bonds with water
Never say esters are formed by 'dehydration' — always use 'condensation reaction'
Students forget that fermentation requires absence of air/oxygen
examinerTips: Esterification appears in almost every Paper 2 — learn the mechanism
Always include concentrated H₂SO₄ as catalyst in esterification equations
Colour changes with dichromate are frequently tested — orange to green
Draw structural formulas for esters showing the C-O-C=O arrangement clearly
note_data.keyDiagramDescription: Structural formula of ester showing R-COO-R' arrangement with double bonded oxygen on carbon and single bonded oxygen linking to alkyl group
